title: World Map — Room System
description: Create a Zone class containing multiple Room objects. Room has: name, description, exits (dict mapping direction to room_name), items (list), npcs (list), and players_here (set). Create a World class with add_zone(zone), get_room(room_name), and move_player(player_name, direction, current_room) that validates the exit exists and moves the player between rooms. World loads rooms from zone definitions.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 3: World Map
assert 'Room' in dir(), 'Room class must be defined'
assert 'Zone' in dir(), 'Zone class must be defined'
assert 'World' in dir(), 'World class must be defined'

# Test Room constructor
room = Room('town_square', 'A bustling town square.', {'north': 'tavern', 'east': 'market'}, ['sword'], ['guard'])
assert room.name == 'town_square', 'Room should store name'
assert room.description == 'A bustling town square.', 'Room should store description'
assert room.exits == {'north': 'tavern', 'east': 'market'}, 'Room should store exits'
assert room.items == ['sword'], 'Room should store items'
assert room.npcs == ['guard'], 'Room should store npcs'
assert isinstance(room.players_here, set), 'players_here should be a set'
assert len(room.players_here) == 0, 'players_here should start empty'

# Test Room defaults
room2 = Room('empty', 'An empty room.')
assert room2.exits == {}, 'exits should default to empty dict'
assert room2.items == [], 'items should default to empty list'
assert room2.npcs == [], 'npcs should default to empty list'

# Test Room player tracking
room.add_player('Alice')
assert 'Alice' in room.players_here, 'Alice should be in the room'
room.add_player('Bob')
assert len(room.players_here) == 2, 'Should have 2 players'
room.remove_player('Alice')
assert 'Alice' not in room.players_here, 'Alice should be removed'
room.remove_player('Nobody')  # Should not error

# Test Room exits
assert room.get_exit('north') == 'tavern', 'north exit should be tavern'
assert room.get_exit('west') is None, 'west exit should be None'

# Test Zone
zone = Zone('town')
assert zone.name == 'town', 'Zone should store name'
assert isinstance(zone.rooms, dict), 'Zone.rooms should be a dict'

zone.add_room(room)
assert zone.get_room('town_square') is room, 'Zone should retrieve room by name'
assert zone.get_room('nonexistent') is None, 'Zone should return None for unknown room'

# Test World
world = World()
assert world.room_count == 0, 'World should start with 0 rooms'

# Build a small world
tavern = Room('tavern', 'A cozy tavern.', {'south': 'town_square'})
market = Room('market', 'A busy market.', {'west': 'town_square'})
town_zone = Zone('town')
town_zone.add_room(Room('town_square', 'A bustling square.', {'north': 'tavern', 'east': 'market'}))
town_zone.add_room(tavern)
town_zone.add_room(market)

world.add_zone(town_zone)
assert world.room_count == 3, f'World should have 3 rooms, got {world.room_count}'
assert world.get_room('tavern') is tavern, 'World should find tavern'
assert world.get_room('unknown') is None, 'World should return None for unknown room'

# Test move_player
square = world.get_room('town_square')
square.add_player('Hero')
result = world.move_player('Hero', 'north', 'town_square')
assert result is not None, 'move_player should return destination room for valid move'
assert result.name == 'tavern', 'Hero should end up in tavern'
assert 'Hero' not in square.players_here, 'Hero should be removed from town_square'
assert 'Hero' in tavern.players_here, 'Hero should be in tavern'

# Test invalid move
bad_result = world.move_player('Hero', 'west', 'tavern')
assert bad_result is None, 'move_player should return None for invalid direction'
assert 'Hero' in tavern.players_here, 'Hero should still be in tavern after invalid move'
acceptance_criteria: Room stores name, description, exits dict, items list, npcs list, and players_here set
Room.add_player() and remove_player() manage the players_here set
Room.get_exit(direction) returns the destination room name or None
Zone stores a name and a dict of rooms, with add_room and get_room
World.add_zone() indexes all rooms for flat lookup
World.get_room() retrieves any room by name across all zones
World.move_player() validates exits and moves player between rooms
hints: Use 'exits=None' in Room.__init__ and set self.exits = exits or {} to avoid mutable default.
Room.remove_player should use self.players_here.discard(name) to avoid KeyError.
In World.add_zone(), loop through zone.rooms.values() and add each to self._rooms.
In move_player(), get the current room, check get_exit(direction), then get the destination room.
